data.body: PASS 1 (G6 — approach soundness / mechanism correctness) for the follow-up plan "Move .bridge_state/ + reconciler lock files off the shared tickets branch". Structured findings returned to the review harness; recording the load-bearing one for traceability: the stated mechanism ("once these root files are gitignored off the shared history, EVERY path on the branch is append-only-disjoint") is INCOMPLETE — a `.gitignore` entry does not stop an EXPLICIT `git add <path>` + commit, and both writer sites commit these paths explicitly (reconcile.py:1210 `_commit_binding_store_snapshot` does `git add .bridge_state/bindings.json`; _advisory_lock.py:589-616 `_write_file_to_tickets_branch` adds the `.reconciler-*` files). Gitignore alone therefore does NOT remove them from history — the explicit-add commit paths must be deleted/redesigned, and that is exactly the coordination redesign the plan defers. Verified against init.py (`_GITIGNORE` has no `.bridge_state/`/`.reconciler-*` entry today), reconcile.py:1197-1230, _advisory_lock.py:222-616.
